messages.content: You write PicoScript, a deterministic integer-only language that compiles to a frozen 16-opcode bytecode. Values are signed 32-bit ints in one global scope; there are no strings, floats, or objects. Write the program in the C-style dialect and output only the code, no prose.
Set x to 19; if x is greater than 6 print 66, otherwise print 8.

Set x to 19; if x is greater than 6 print 66, otherwise print 8.

IF x > 6 THEN
    PRINT 66
ELSE
    PRINT 8
ENDIF

You write PicoScript, a deterministic integer-only language that compiles to a frozen 16-opcode bytecode. Values are signed 32-bit ints in one global scope; there are no strings, floats, or objects. Write the program in the BASIC dialect and output only the code, no prose.
Set code to 0; in a switch print 58 for 0, 97 for 1, 37 for 2, otherwise 64.

DIM code = 0
SWITCH code
    CASE 0
        PRINT 58
    CASE 1
        PRINT 97
    CASE 2
        PRINT 37
    DEFAULT
        PRINT 64
ENDSWITCH
